# DiSCO: Differentiable Scan Context With Orientation

- **Work ID:** `LPR-057`
- **Reviewed version:** `arxiv:2010.10949`
- **Year / venue:** 2021 / RAL
- **Relevance:** `core_lpr`
- **Representative tier:** `ordinary`

## Accepted structured facts

- **datasets:** Oxford RobotCar/PointNetVLAD benchmark, NCLT, MulRan — `source_fact`; manual, p. 5–6, § IV-A. Dataset and Experimental Settings, Fig. 5;Table II
- **input_unit:** single ground-removed 3D LiDAR scan, Oxford experiments use a 4096-point submap — `source_fact`; manual, p. 3–6, § III-A. Problem Statement;IV-A. Dataset and Experimental Settings, Fig. 2-3
- **invariances:** yaw rotation (theoretical for pure rotation, approximate under translation and environmental change), limited pitch robustness (multi-layer BEV only) — `researcher_judgement`; manual, p. 1–9, § Abstract;III-A. Problem Statement;III-B. Differentiable Signature;III-C. Metric Learning;Appendix C. Pitch Problem, Eq. 1-5;Fig. 3;Fig. 10
- **representation:** polar multi-layer occupied/density BEV or single-layer height BEV, U-Net features, FFT-magnitude low-frequency signature, Euclidean-distance KD-tree retrieval, shared differentiable phase correlation and softmax-expectation yaw estimation, joint quadruplet and KL-divergence losses — `source_fact`; manual, p. 3–5, § III-B. Differentiable Signature in Frequency Domain;III-C. Metric Learning for Place Recognition;III-D. Differentiable Relative Angle Estimation;III-E. Implementation and Training, Fig. 2-4;Eq. 4-15
- **uses_learning:** yes — `source_fact`; manual, p. 3–5, § III-B. Differentiable Signature in Frequency Domain;III-C. Metric Learning;III-E. Implementation and Training, Eq. 4-6;Eq. 14-15
- **uses_sequence:** no — `researcher_judgement`; manual, p. 1–6, § Abstract;III. Methodology;IV. Experiments, Fig. 1-2
